stem: Fixed-point iteration is applied to \( g(x) = [[r]]x + ([[d]]) \), starting from \( x_0 = [[x0]] \), using \( x_{k+1} = g(x_k) \). What is \( x_{[[n]]} \)?
solution: Apply \( g \) repeatedly starting from \( x_0=[[x0]] \); after [[n]] iterations, \( x_{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the additive constant, treating the map as purely multiplicative.
This treats the map as purely additive, ignoring the multiplicative factor \( r \).
This applies \( g \) only ONCE instead of \( n \) times.
This applies \( g \) one time too few before adding the last constant term.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.

stem: To approximate \( \sqrt{[[k]]} \), fixed-point (Newton / Babylonian) iteration is applied to \( g(x) = \dfrac{1}{2}\left(x + \dfrac{[[k]]}{x}\right) \), starting from \( x_0 = [[x0]] \). What is \( x_{[[n]]} \) (as an exact fraction)?
solution: Apply \( g(x)=\frac{1}{2}(x+k/x) \) repeatedly from \( x_0=[[x0]] \); after [[n]] iterations, \( x_{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \) (as an exact fraction, not yet the limiting decimal value).
distractor_rationales: This applies \( g \) only ONCE instead of \( n \) times.
This uses a single linear-correction step instead of iterating the actual update rule.
This reports the true irrational limit \( \sqrt{k} \) rather than the exact rational iterate.
This raises \( x_0 \) to the \( n \)-th power inside the formula instead of actually iterating \( n \) times.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.

stem: Fixed-point iteration is applied to \( g(x) = \dfrac{[[k]]}{x + [[d]]} \), starting from \( x_0 = [[x0]] \), using \( x_{j+1} = g(x_j) \). What is \( x_{[[n]]} \) (as an exact fraction)?
solution: Apply \( g(x)=\dfrac{k}{x+d} \) repeatedly from \( x_0=[[x0]] \); after [[n]] iterations, \( x_{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This applies \( g \) only ONCE instead of \( n \) times.
This incorrectly scales the shift \( d \) by \( n \) instead of actually iterating \( n \) times.
This ignores the map entirely and just shifts \( x_0 \) by \( n \).
This raises the numerator to the \( n \)-th power instead of actually iterating \( n \) times.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.

stem: Newton's method is used to find a root of \( f(x) = x^2 - [[c]]x - [[k]] \) via the fixed-point iteration \( x_{j+1} = x_j - \dfrac{f(x_j)}{f'(x_j)} \), starting from \( x_0 = [[x0]] \). What is \( x_{[[n]]} \) (as an exact fraction)?
solution: Apply Newton's update \( x - f(x)/f'(x) \) repeatedly from \( x_0=[[x0]] \); after [[n]] iterations, \( x_{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This applies Newton's update only ONCE instead of \( n \) times.
This forgets to divide by the derivative \( f'(x) \) before subtracting.
This just reports \( f(x_0) \), not an updated iterate at all.
This adds the correction term instead of subtracting it.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.
